claim_boundary.not_proved_by_design: No second-product archive, manifest, product lock, target behavior, upgrade, downgrade, compatibility, migration, or recovery transition is proved.
claim_boundary.proved_by_design: The repository selects a genuine v3.14.5 source-native producer input and a six-stage authority-acquisition contract.

requirements.statement: Second product is CPython 3.14.5, the immediate stable predecessor of first product 3.14.6.
Second product uses exact upstream tag v3.14.5 at commit 5607950ef232dad16d75c0cf53101d9649d89115.
Target host remains aarch64-linux-android.
Android API remains 24.
NDK remains 27.3.13750724 unless a separate authority decision changes it.
Official Python.org source and Android packages are reference identities, not the project product authority.
The exact v3.14.5 Android/android.py and Android/android-env.sh blobs are captured.
Dependency release tags are parsed from the exact v3.14.5 Android/android.py.
All six dependency assets are downloaded and independently hashed before replay.
OpenSSL 3.0.20-0 is treated as a real product-input delta from first-product OpenSSL 3.5.7-0.
Source archive/tag/worktree identity is verified without local source edits.
Build host, Android SDK/NDK, compiler, linker, make, and Python tool identities are captured.
Replay starts from an isolated clean source worktree and empty cross-build/output roots.
The source-native Android producer performs dependency unpack, build-Python, target configure, target build, install, and package steps.
No first-product payload bytes are copied into the second product candidate.
The produced candidate reports Python 3.14.5 and exact expected target/platform identity.
Candidate packaging preserves exact replay archive and extracted prefix identities.
Runtime-base, development-addon, and test-addon are complete and independently owned artifacts.
Each manifest has canonical paths, types, modes, sizes or link targets, and SHA-256 for regular files.
The second product lock and manifest index refer only to second-product artifact identities.
The ownership registry template preserves the frozen registry schema and disjoint artifact ownership.
Artifact reconstruction equals the complete replay candidate prefix under the accepted exclusion policy.
Second-product validation uses fresh inode-separated Termux roots and exact input hashes.
Runtime CLI, stdlib native imports, HTTPS trust, subprocess, venv, uv explicit interpreter, and relocation behavior are validated.
Both addons install independently over exact runtime-base and remain behaviorally valid.
Same-version reinstall, repair boundaries, preservation, recovery, lock exclusion, and final teardown are validated as standalone second-product properties.
No upgrade or downgrade operation is executed in second-product standalone authority acquisition.
A complete result archive is produced on pass or fail with raw return codes and logs.
Root and nested result indexes are complete and independently recomputed.
Archive members are safe: no absolute/traversal paths, unsafe links, or special entries.
Independent audit recomputes artifact, manifest, product lock, ownership, native closure, and runtime evidence.
Second product is frozen only after repository design, workstation replay/materialization, Termux validation, and external audit all pass.
Gate 3B, Gate 3C, and Gate 3D first-product authorities remain immutable inputs.
A1 design does not create or freeze a second-product artifact authority.
No mixed-version compatibility, transition order, collision, recovery, upgrade, or downgrade policy is frozen.
Gate 4 transition design starts only after A6 independent freeze.

stages.name: selection-and-design
stages.objective: Select CPython 3.14.5 and freeze the source-native producer boundary.

stages.name: input-capture
stages.objective: Capture exact source, producer, dependency assets, NDK/toolchain, host tools, and download archive inventories.

stages.name: upstream-replay
stages.objective: Replay the exact v3.14.5 Android producer from a clean source worktree without editing product identity.

stages.name: three-artifact-materialization
stages.objective: Derive complete runtime-base, development-addon, and test-addon artifacts with manifests, product lock, manifest index, and ownership template.

stages.name: standalone-target-validation
stages.objective: Install and validate the second product independently on fresh Termux roots, including runtime, native closure, addons, relocation, reinstall, recovery, and final teardown.

stages.name: external-audit-and-freeze
stages.objective: Recompute archive identities, indexes, safety, manifests, ownership counts, behavior evidence, and claim boundary before freezing authority.
